content.full_text:
LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT

OF

ELBERT A. READ

I, Elbert A. Read, of Ehourandah, Iowa, being of sound and disposing
mind and memory, do hereby make, publish and declare this to be my Last Will
and Testament, hereby revoking any and all wills and testamentary papers
heretofore made by me.

ITEM I

I nominate and appoint my daughter, Marion Read Ambler of Waco, Texas
as Executrix of this, my Last Will and Testament, and I direct that no bond or other security be required from either
of said executors while serving in such capacity.

ITEM II

I direct my Executors to pay all my just debts and funeral expenses,
as well as all the costs and expenses of the administration of my estate, as
soon after my death as shall be practicable, provided, however, that such
direction shall not be construed as a waiver of any limitation, exemption
or homestead right; and provided, further, that my Executors shall not be
required to accelerate payment of any debt secured by mortgage on real
estate but shall distribute such real estate subject to the mortgage.

ITEM III

I give and bequeath to my said wife, Jossbol Read, if she survives
me by a period of at least six months, all or any part that she shall not
already own of our household furniture, goods and effects, including linen,
silverware, books, objects of art, and all other articles intended for use
in or ornamentation of our home, and all my wearing apparel and articles of
personal adornment, jewelry and automobiles; provided that in case my wife
shall not survive me by a period of at least six months, then I give and
bequeath the same in equal shares to my children who survive me.

ITEM IV

I hereby give, devise and bequeath all of the rest, residue and remainder
of my estate, both real and personal, of every kind, nature or description,
and wheresoever the same may be situated, IN TRUST to my trustees, hereinafter
named, for the use and benefit of each separate trust as hereinafter specified,
and for the purposes set upon and upon the trust terms and conditions hereinafter set forth.

ARTICLE I

The trust period for each separate trust created hereunder
shall commence upon the transfer of the trust property to the
trustees, and shall continue during the lives of my daughters,
Marion Read Ambler and [illegible].

and my grandchildren who shall survive me, and the survivor
of them, and for twenty-one (21) years thereafter, unless
sooner terminated as hereinafter provided.

ARTICLE II

As soon after my death as is practicable and lawful, the
property whose described shall be divided by my Executors into
as many equal trust funds as there shall be daughters of mine
who survive me or shall have predeceased me leaving children
who survive me.

In making a division of the property provided for in this
Article, the Executors shall value each item of property and shall
divide the same into substantially equal portions in value, and
such executors shall not be required to assign or allot to each
trust its interest in any separate parcel or item of property,
but may assign and transfer one parcel or item of property to
one trust and another item or parcel of property to the other
trust, or may assign and transfer an undivided interest in and
to an integral parcel or item of property to both trusts and
each separate trust may hold and own an undivided part interest
in and to integral properties. In making such division, the
judgment of the Executors as to values to be placed upon the
different kinds of property shall be conclusive and final.

ARTICLE III

As to each trust created by Article II for a daughter
who survives me, the net income and principal shall be distributed as follows:

Section 1.

During the life of my daughter the Trustee shall,
in his sole discretion, expend so much of the income and
principal of the trust as he may deem wise and prudent
for the care, support and maintenance of the daughter,
provided that payments made from principal in any one
year shall not exceed ten per cent (10%) thereof. Any
income from the trust not distributed in any year shall
be accumulated and added to and become a part of the
principal of the trust.

Section 2.

Upon the death of a daughter of mine who is living
at my death the remaining principal of the fund created

-2-

above named for her, together with all accrued or undis-
tributed net income therefrom, shall be distributed to or
for the benefit of such persons or organizations in such
proportions and subject to such trusts, powers and con-
ditions as such daughter may provide and appoint by will,
except that such daughter shall not have the power to
appoint to or for the benefit of herself, her estate,
or the creditors of either. The acting trustee shall
be fully protected in relying upon an instrument which
has been admitted to probate in any jurisdiction as the
last will of a daughter of mine, or if the trustee has
no actual notice of the existence of a will of such
daughter within three months after her death, the trustee
may presume such daughter died without a will.

To the extent the power of appointment set forth
in this section is not exercised any portion so undis-
tributed shall be distributed outright in such proportions
and to those heirs of my daughter as shall be determined
as though she had died intestate, but in accordance with
the laws of the State of Iowa in effect as of the date of
this will, relating to the descent of personal property or
intestate decedents.

ARTICLE IV

As to each trust created by Article II for the children of
a daughter of mine who shall have predeceased me the trustee shall
divide the principal into as many equal shares as there are sur-
viving children of such deceased daughter and the income and
principal of each such share shall be distributed as follows:

Section 1.

During the life of the beneficiary the trustee in his
sole discretion shall expend so much of the income and
principal of said share as he may deem wise and prudent for
the care support, maintenance and education of the beneficiary.
Any income not distributed shall be accumulated and added to
and become a part of the principal.

Section 2.

When the beneficiary attains the age of thirty (30)
years, he shall receive outright absolutely one-third (1/3)
in value of the principal and accumulated income of his or
her share then being held in trust; when he attains or has
attained the age of thirty-five (35) years, he shall receive

principal and accumulated income of his or her share
then being held in trust; and when he attains or has
attained the age of forty (40) years, he or she shall
receive outright and absolutely the remainder of the
principal and accumulated income then being held in his or
her share and the trust as to that share shall terminate.

Section 3.

If the beneficiary shall die before he has attained
the age of forty (40) years leaving descendants, then
the remainder of the deceased beneficiary's share shall
then be distributed, per stirpes and not per capita to
his descendants.

Section 4.

If the beneficiary shall die before he has attained
the age of forty (40) years, leaving no descendants the
remainder of the share of such deceased beneficiary shall
be distributed outright and in such proportions and to
those heirs of said beneficiary of such deceased beneficiary
as shall be determined as though he died intestate, but in
accordance with the laws of the State of Iowa in effect as
of the date of this will, relating to the descent of per-
sonal property or intestate descendants.

ARTICLE V

Notwithstanding any contrary provision of this
Article, if and in case the statutory law of any state in which
any property of the trust is held shall impose legally effective
restrictions upon the accumulation of income by a Trustee from
property contrary to the accumulation of income herein provided
for or permitted, such Trustee shall pay each year to the person
or persons then entitled by the terms hereof to receive payments
of income thereto at such discretion of such Trustee such not income as
it shall be unlawful to accumulate.

ARTICLE VI

No title in the trust or trusts created in this item or in
any property at any time becoming a part of any such trust, or in
the income therefrom shall vest in any beneficiary, and neither
the principal nor the income of any such trust estate shall be
liable to be reached in any manner by the creditors of any bene-
ficiary, and no beneficiary shall have any power to sell, assign,
transfer, encumber, or in any other manner to anticipate or dispose

of his or her interest in any such trust estate, or the income
produced thorby prior to the actual distribution by the
trustee to the beneficiary.

ARTICLE VII

The Trustees of each separate trust shall be as follows:

Section 1.

[illegible] of Darien, Connecticut shall be
the original trustee of the trust created for my daughter,
[illegible] or in the event that she be not
living at the time of my death, for her children. In
the event that the said [illegible] is unwilling or
unable to serve I nominate and appoint [illegible] of
New York, New York as alternate or successor trustee.

Section 2.

[illegible] of Waco, Texas, shall be the
original trustee of the trust created for my daughter,
[illegible] or in the event she be not living
at the time of my death, for her children.

Section 3.

[illegible] of Waco, Texas shall be the
original trustee of the trust created for my daughter,
Marion Reed Ambler, or in the event she be not living
at the time of my death, for her children.

Section 4.

Any of the persons designated above as original
trustees may, should he or she desire, appoint a corporate
trustee to act with him or her as co-trustee.

Section 5.

None of the persons designated above as original
trustee, or successor trustees, nor any corporate trustee
appointed by an original trustee to act as co-trustee
shall be required to furnish bond or other security while
serving in such capacity.

ARTICLE VIII

My trustees shall have power and authority to do any act or
thing reasonably necessary or advisable for the proper administration

and distribution of each trust created by this Will. In extension
but not in limitation of any power, right, or discretion otherwise
possessed by my trustees, I grant to them, without the necessity
of notice to or approval of any court or person, the following
powers:

Section 1.

To retain any asset, regardless of whether it is of the
kind authorized by this Will for investment and whether it
leaves a disproportionately large part of my estate invested
in one type of property; for so long as they deem advisable,
and to sell, exchange, mortgage, lease, or otherwise dispose
of any asset for terms within or extending beyond the terms
of the trust, and to receive from any source additional
properties acceptable to them.

Section 2.

To acquire, invest, reinvest, exchange, retain, sell,
and manage trust assets, exercising the judgment and care
under the circumstances then prevailing which men of prudence,
discretion, and intelligence exercise in the management of
their own affairs, not in regard to speculation but in regard
to the permanent disposition of their funds, considering the
probable income as well as the probable safety of their capital.
Within the limitations of that standard my trustees are author-
ized to acquire and retain every kind of property, real, per-
sonal, or mixed, and every kind of investment, specifically
including but not by way of limitation, bonds, debentures,
and other corporate obligations, and stocks, preferred or
common, which men of prudence, discretion, and intelligence
acquire or retain for their own account.

Section 3.

To collect, receive, and receipt for any principal or
income, and to enforce, defend against, prosecute, compromise,
or settle any claim by or against the trust.

Section 4.

To vote, execute proxies to vote, join in or oppose
any plans for reorganization, and exercise any other rights
incident to the ownership of any stocks, bonds, or other
properties of the trust estate.

Section 5.

To hold assets in bearer form or in the name of the


-6-

corporate trustee or in the name of its nominees or nominees
without disclosing any fiduciary relationship provided,
however, that all assets and cash shall at all times appear
as a part of the trust on the books of the corporate trustee.

Section 6.

To make division or distribution, whenever required by
this will, in whole or in part in money, securities, or
other property, and in undivided interests therein, and to
continue to hold any remaining undivided interest in any
trust hereunder. In any division or distribution the judgment of the trustees concerning the propriety thereof and
the valuation of the properties and securities concerned
shall be binding and conclusive on all persons in interest.

Section 7.

To make payments to or for the benefit of any beneficiary
(specifically including any beneficiary under any legal disability) in any of the following ways: (1) Directly to the
beneficiary; (2) directly for the maintenance, welfare, and
education of the beneficiary; (3) to the legal or natural
guardian of the beneficiary; or (4) to anyone who at the
time shall have custody and care of the person of the beneficiary. The trustees shall not be obliged to see to the
application of the funds so paid, but the receipt of the
person to whom the funds were paid shall be full acquittance
of the trustees.

Section 8.

To determine what is principal and what is income of any
trust and, in their uncontrolled discretion, to allocate or
apportion receipts and expenses between principal and income,
except that all premiums shall be charged and all discounts
credited against or to principal, as the case may be, and not
against or to income, and shall be amortized; and except,
further, that all dividends paid in stock of the paying corporation shall be considered principal.

Section 9.

To employ investment counsel, accountants, lawyers, and
agents and to charge the cost of such services to principal
or income.

Section 10.

To hold the assets of the several trusts, shares, or

-7-

portions of trusts created by this will as a single fund
for joint investment and management, without the need for
physical segregation, dividing the incom proportionately
among them. Segregation of the various trusts, shares, or
portions need only be made on the books of the corporate
trustee for accounting purposes.

Section 11.

To receive on behalf of the corporate trustee reasonable
compensation for its services under this will, and to pay all
reasonable expenses and charges of the trust or trusts.

ARTICLE IX

The Trustees may, from time to time, select and determine the
principal place of business of each corporate trust and all intangible
personal property, including (but not limited to) stocks, bonds,
notes, contracts, mortgages and leases owned by each corporate trust,
shall be held and kept at the principal place of business so selected
and determined.

ITEM V

The fact that, other than in the provisions in ITEM III, I have made
no provision in this will for my wife, Jesabel Read, is not because of
lack affection but for the reason that she has an adequate estate of her
own. My said wife has full knowledge of this will and concurs fully in all
of its provisions. However, if my said wife should disclaim her statutory
share of my estate, the balance of my estate shall nevertheless to subject
to the provisions of this will which shall be carried out insofar as possible.

ITEM VI

I give and grant to my executors with respect to my estate all powers,
duties, and discretions given and granted to my trustees with respect to
my trust estates, and which shall be in addition to and not in limitation
of those which my executors otherwise would possess.

ITEM VII

I hereby declare there is no mutuality of consignment or agreement
between myself and my wife, Jesabel Read, in connection with the making,
publishing and declaration of this instrument as my Last Will and Testament,
and any making, publishing and declaration by my said wife of her last Will
and Testament.

ITEM VIII

The foregoing provisions of this Will shall be supplemented by and when


-8-

necessary shall be subject to the following:

Section 1. The expressions "children" and "descendants" shall include
legally adopted children and descendants, but do not include illegitimate
children and descendants. The plural expressions "trustees," "children,"
"descendants," "beneficiaries," and "persons" shall include the singular
and the contexts of this instrument shall be read accordingly when the facts
require it. Likewise, any singular noun shall include the plural and the
context of this instrument shall be read accordingly when the facts require
it.

Section 2. The masculine pronoun shall be taken to refer to the person
or persons intended, regardless of number or gender, and the singular
pronoun try to taken to express a plural pronoun, and a plural pronoun may
be taken to express a singular pronoun when the facts require it.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I hereunto set my hand and in the presence of
three witnesses declare this to be my Last Will and Testament this _______
day of June, 1959.

                                                          Testator

On this _____ day of June, 1959, the foregoing instrument consisting
of the and the eight (8) preceding pages, each page being typewritten
and initialed on the margin by the testator, except this last page which
was signed at the end by him, was published and declared by Elbert A. Redd,
the above-named testator, as and for his Last Will and Testament, in the
eight and presence of us and each of us, who thereupon at his request and
in his sight and presence, and in the sight and presence of each other,
have hereunto subscribed our names as witnesses, and have set down our
place of residence opposite our names.

_________________________________Residing at__________________________

_________________________________Residing at__________________________

_________________________________Residing at__________________________


                                    -9-
